input.prompt:
I have a .NET MAUI ContentPage called SettingsPage.xaml that currently uses
plain bindings with no x:DataType. The page's BindingContext is a
SettingsViewModel with properties: string Theme, bool NotificationsEnabled,
and int FontSize. Convert this page to use compiled bindings and tell me
how to catch binding errors at build time.

data.content: No files found in the working directory — I'll create a working example showing the conversion and explain how to catch errors at build time.

arguments.file_text:
<?xml version="1.0" encoding="utf-8" ?>
<ContentPage xmlns="http://schemas.microsoft.com/dotnet/2021/maui"
             xmlns:x="http://schemas.microsoft.com/winfx/2009/xaml"
             xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
             x:Class="MyApp.Views.SettingsPage"
             x:DataType="vm:SettingsViewModel">

    <VerticalStackLayout Padding="20" Spacing="15">

        <Label Text="Settings" FontSize="24" FontAttributes="Bold" />

        <!-- Theme -->
        <Label Text="Theme" />
        <Entry Text="{Binding Theme}" />

        <!-- Notifications -->
        <Label Text="Enable Notifications" />
        <Switch IsToggled="{Binding NotificationsEnabled}" />

        <!-- Font Size -->
        <Label Text="Font Size" />
        <Label Text="{Binding FontSize, StringFormat='Current size: {0}'}" />
        <Stepper Value="{Binding FontSize}" Minimum="8" Maximum="32" Increment="1" />

    </VerticalStackLayout>

</ContentPage>

data.content:
Here's the converted page. Two key changes enable compiled bindings:

### 1. Add `x:DataType` to the root element
```xml
xmlns:vm="clr-namespace:MyApp.ViewModels"
x:DataType="vm:SettingsViewModel"
```
This tells the XAML compiler the type of `BindingContext`, so every `{Binding ...}` is verified against `SettingsViewModel` at compile time.

### 2. Catch errors at build time
Compiled bindings are **validated during compilation** — if you typo a property (e.g., `{Binding Themee}`), you get a **build error**, not a silent runtime failure.

To ensure this is fully enforced, add to your `.csproj`:
```xml
<MauiStrictXamlCompilation>true</MauiStrictXamlCompilation>
```

This promotes XAML binding warnings (like `XC0045`: property not found on type) to **errors**, so your CI pipeline will catch them.

### Quick summary of what changed vs. plain bindings
| Before | After |
|---|---|
| No `x:DataType` | `x:DataType="vm:SettingsViewModel"` on `<ContentPage>` |
| Bindings resolved at runtime via reflection | Bindings compiled to direct property access (faster, type-safe) |
| Typos fail silently at runtime | Typos caught at build time |
